introduction:
In Madhya Pradesh, the Jati Praman Patra (Caste Certificate) is issued by the Tehsildar / SDM (Sub-Divisional Magistrate) under the Revenue Department for SC, ST, OBC, and EWS applicants.

Citizens can apply online via the MP e-District Portal (mpedistrict.gov.in) or at any Lok Seva Kendra (LSK) / Common Service Centre. Madhya Pradesh guarantees service delivery under the MP Lok Seva Guarantee Act within defined timelines.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Issued by Tehsildar / SDM of the applicant's native tehsil
  • Processing time: 7–21 working days (guaranteed under MP Lok Seva Guarantee Act)
  • Fee: ₹20–₹40 (via e-District / LSK)
  • SC/ST certificates are permanent; OBC certificates valid for 3 years
  • Online portal: MP e-District Portal (mpedistrict.gov.in)
  • Service guaranteed under MP Lok Seva Guarantee Act, 2010
documentsRequired:
Documents Required

Identity Proof
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Voter ID
  • Ration Card
  • Passport
Caste / Nativity Proof
  • Father's Caste Certificate
  • Class X / School Certificate showing caste
  • Village Land Records / Khasra (native village)
  • Old Ration Card with caste entry
Additional (OBC)
  • Income Certificate (Non-Creamy Layer — ₹8 lakh limit)
  • Domicile Certificate (MP)
  • Passport-size photographs (2)
  • Self-declaration affidavit
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via MP e-District Portal

Apply at MP e-District Portal

  1. Visit Portal
    Go to mpedistrict.gov.in
  2. Register / Login
    Register using your mobile number and Aadhaar. Verify via OTP.
  3. Select Service
    Navigate to Revenue Services → Jati Praman Patra (Caste Certificate).
  4. Fill Application
    Enter applicant name, DOB, caste, tehsil, district, and native village details accurately.
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload scanned copies of required documents in PDF/JPG format.
  6. Pay Fee
    Pay ₹20–₹40 online via UPI / Debit Card / Net Banking.
  7. Submit & Track
    Submit and track using your application/acknowledgement number.
  8. Download Certificate
    Download the digitally signed caste certificate once approved by the Tehsildar.
Applications can also be submitted at any Lok Seva Kendra (LSK) or Common Service Centre (CSC) near you.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at Tehsil / Lok Seva Kendra

  1. Visit Tehsil or Lok Seva Kendra
    Go to your native tehsil office or the nearest Lok Seva Kendra (LSK) in Madhya Pradesh.
  2. Collect & Fill Form
    Obtain the Jati Praman Patra application form and fill all details.
  3. Submit with Documents
    Submit self-attested document copies with the form and pay the fee at the counter.
  4. Patwari Verification
    The Patwari (Village Revenue Officer) will verify caste and native village records.
  5. Collect Certificate
    Collect the caste certificate from the Tehsildar office within the guaranteed timeline (7–21 working days).
Processing Time: 7–21 working days under MP Lok Seva Guarantee Act, 2010.
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Track Application

Track at MP e-District Portal using your acknowledgement number.

OBC Renewal

OBC certificates are valid for 3 years. Renew via e-District or LSK before expiry.

Grievance / Delay

Call 181 (MP Helpline) or raise a complaint at MP Samadhan Portal. If service delayed beyond guaranteed period, escalate under Lok Seva Guarantee Act.
